observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the
Municipal Accountant to:
a. Prepare and submit monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements on time in
order to check the accuracy of the balances of the bank accounts and to
submit the journal entry vouchers to ensure proper recording of all valid
reconciling items that require adjustment/correction in the General
Ledger.
b. Make a formal request to the depository bank for the early release of
bank statements with corresponding debit/credit advices so that Bank
Reconciliation Statements could be timely prepared.
c. Conduct periodic reconciliation with the Municipal Treasurer of the
Cash in Bank balances per books and per cashbooks at least once at the
end of each quarter, so that discrepancies will be immediately traced and
necessary adjustments made.

Recommendations:

a. We reiterated our previous year audit recommendations for the LGU to
conduct periodic physical inventory of its property, plant and
equipment. Instruct the Municipal GSO and Accountant to reconcile the
records with the physical count. Items that are beyond economic repair
shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books. Management
must adhere the PPSAS 17.
b. Management must create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the
overall physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the
office of the auditor immediately thereafter.
c. Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all
PPE, as this activity in very important in checking actual existence of the
assets and determining its usefulness and current condition as well as
pinpoint custodial responsibilities.
d. General Service Office must maintain complete PCs by providing
identification number, issued acknowledgement receipt for equipment
(ARE) and stickers to each property newly purchased or acquired
e. Accounting Office to prepare PPELCs and update them periodically to
aid in the conduct of Physical Inventory Count and in the reconciliation
of balances between the Inventory Report and the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Assessor to prepare and submit the taxpayer’s assessment roll to the Municipal
Treasurer for use in the preparation of duly certified list of taxpayers and the
amount due thereon to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant. Then the
Municipal Accountant shall set-up, thru the preparation of JEV at the
beginning of the year the estimated receivable of RPT and SET.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant and Treasurer be instructed to
strictly adhere the guidelines pertaining to the submission of financial
statements and DVs and other accounting reports. Instruct the head of the
accounting section to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel,
so as to prevent work overload, meet deadlines on reports submission and to
ensure ample time for the audit and also to provide the agency of the needed
information which could be vital in their decision making.

Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and
Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions
and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to the
Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.

We recommended that Management should require the Municipal
Treasurer/Property Officer to prepare the report of properties that require
insurance coverage and submit the same to the GSIS.

Further, management should insure all insurable assets with the General
Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss
due to fire and other contingent event in accordance with to Republic Act No.
656, otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by PD No. 245
dated July 13, 1973.

Management should insure all insurable assets with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss due to fire and other contingent event in accordance with RA No. 656, otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by PD No. 245 dated July 13, 1973.
observations.managementAction: We will communicate required documents in order we can insure the Municipal Buildings.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended that Management adhere to the pertinent provisions of the
Local Government Code and cause the Sangguniang Bayan to enact updated
ordinances that can be the basis of the general revision of real property
assessment and classification once every three (3 )years to maximize LGU’s
effort in raising local revenues.
a. Continuously coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and collaborate an
action plan in the implementation of strategies in the collection of Real
Property Tax (RPT) by the municipalities.
b. Persistently follow up and closely monitor the concerned municipality to
keep track on the full remittances of the provincial share from the total
Real Property Tax due and expedite the collection of delinquent RPT.
c. Consider the remedies available in Republic Act No. 7160 to improve
collections efficiency.

Recommendation:
a. We reiterate our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical
inventory of its property, plant and equipment. Items that are beyond
economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.
Management must adhere the PPSAS 17 .
b. Management must create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the
overall physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the
office of the auditor immediately thereafter.
c. Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE,
as this activity in very important in checking actual existence of the assets
and determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint
custodial responsibilities.
d. Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment Ledger
Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and
AAR_Municipality of Sapa Sapa CY2020 Page 29
likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect
complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.
e. Require the accountable officers of the LGU to update the physical
inventory of PPE in adherence to Section 63 to 74, GAM-LGU Vol. II and
submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) to the Auditor
not later than January 31 of each year.

Recommendation:
Management should create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of
unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with the GSIS on the
interest due. Thereafter, they should come up with a commitment on how to
remit the long outstanding premiums. However, those officials responsible for
not remitting the mandatory contribution should be answerable for their
actions.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Municipal Accountant and Treasurer be instructed to
strictly adhere the guidelines pertaining to the submission of financial statements
and DVs and other accounting reports. Instruct the head of the accounting
section to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to
prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission.
Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and
Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions
and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to the
Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the regulations..

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management should require the Municipal
Treasurer/Property Officer to prepare the report of properties that require
insurance coverage and submit the same to the GSIS.
Further, management should insure all insurable assets with the General
Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss
due to fire and other contingent event in accordance with to Republic Act No.
656, otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by PD No. 245
dated July 13, 1973.

Management should create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with the GSIS on the interest due. Thereafter, they should come up with a commitment on how to remit the long outstanding premiums. However, those officials responsible for not remitting the mandatory contribution should be answerable for their actions.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
a. We reiterate our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical
inventory of its property, plant and equipment. Items that are beyond
economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.
Management must adhere the PPSAS 17 & 27 of Volume I and II
respectively.
b. Management must create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the
overall physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the
office of the auditor immediately thereafter.
c. Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE,
as this activity in very important in checking actual existence of the assets
and determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint
custodial responsibilities.
d. Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment Ledger
Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and
likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect
complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.
e. Require the accountable officers of the LGU to update the physical
inventory of PPE in adherence to Section 63, Manual on the NGAS, Vol.II
and submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) to the
Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.
f. Every accountable officer must adhere and carefully evaluate their
accountability on Section 494,GAAM, Vol. I, which states that “Every
accountable officer for government property shall be liable for its money
value in case of improper or unauthorized use or misapplication thereof, by
himself or by any person for whose acts he maybe responsible. He shall
likewise be liable for all losses, damage or deterioration occasioned by
negligence in the keeping or use of the property, whether or not, it be at the
time in his actual custody (PD 1445).

Recommendation:
We recommend that copies of POs/Contracts and AIR be submitted on time to
the Auditor as required under Section A(1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and
Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

Further, management should insure all insurable assets with the General
Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss

656, otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by PD No. 245
dated July 13, 1973.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) fast tract the implementation of the projects/programs funded by the National Government Agencies so as not deprive further the public of the use and benefits derived from the said projects and follow-up to the concerned NGAs the immediate release of funds.

We recommended that management prepare its 10-year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan with emphasis on the compliance of the requirements and provisions set forth in RA 9003 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations. Prepare annually the action plan on solid waste management that details the priority activities to be performed and delivered for the gradual implementation of its 10-year plan.

We recommended that management prepare its Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP) and Comprehensive Development Plan (CDP) which may lead to unfavourable impact socio-economic and environmental  development as provided under Section 106, 107 & 109  of R.A No. 7160, Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended that to provide information for a complete profile of the Leagues of Local Government Units (LGUs) and the Leagues and Federations of Local Elective Officials pursuant to COA Resolution No. 2011-014 dated December 13, 2011 and COA Memorandum No. 2012-008.

The Audit Team reiterated to direct the  Local Chief Executive to create a Committee to undertake the reconciliation of the Due to GSIS account. Surcharges and penalties for prior accounting periods should be recorded unless these have been condoned by the GSIS. Thereafter, the LGU should come up with a Commitment Sheet on how to remit the long outstanding payable.

observations.recommendation: Management should insure all insurable assets with the General Insurance   Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss due to fire and other contingent event in accordance with Republic Act No. 656, otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by Presidential Decree No. 245 dated July 13, 1973.

Management should create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with the GSIS on the interest due. Thereafter, they should come up with a commitment on how to remit the long outstanding premiums. However, those officials responsible for not remitting the mandatory contribution  should be answerable for their actions.

LGU must submit and furnished copies of contract and memorandum of agreement  and their required attachment within five (5) days from their issuances to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations.

We recommend that copies of Purchase Orders/Contracts and Acceptance and Inspection Reports be submitted on time to the Auditor’s Office as required under Section A (1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We reiterate our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment.  Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books. Management must adhere the PPSAS 17 & 27 of Volume I and II respectively.
Management must create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the office of the auditor immediately thereafter.
Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE, as this activity in very important in checking actual existence of the assets and determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint custodial responsibilities.
Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.
Require the accountable officers of the LGU to update the physical inventory of PPE in adherence to Section 63, Manual on the NGAS, Vol.II and submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.
Every accountable officer must adhere and carefully evaluate their accountability on Section 494, GAAM, Vol. I, which states that “Every accountable officer for government property shall be liable for its money value in case of improper or unauthorized use or misapplication thereof, by himself or by any person for whose acts he maybe responsible. He shall likewise be liable for all losses, damage or deterioration occasioned by negligence in the keeping or use of the property, whether or not, it be at the time in his actual custody (PD 1445).

We recommend that municipal accountant and the municipal treasurer be instructed to strictlyadhere to the guidelines pertaining to the submission of financial statements and disbursement vouchers and other accounting reports. Instruct the head of the accounting section to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission.
Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to the Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) fast tract the implementation of the projects/programs funded by the National Government Agencies so as not deprive further the public of the use and benefits derived
from the said projects and follow-up to the concerned NGAs the immediate release of funds.

We recommended that management prepare its 10-year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan with emphasis on the compliance of the requirements and provisions set forth in RA 9003 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations. Prepare annually the action plan on solid waste management that details the priority activities to be performed and delivered for the gradual implementation of its 10-year plan.

We recommended that management prepare its Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP) and Comprehensive Development Plan (CDP) which may lead to unfavourable impact socio-economic and environmental  development as provided under Section 106, 107 & 109  of R.A No. 7160, Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended that to provide information for a complete profile of the Leagues of Local Government Units (LGUs) and the Leagues and Federations of Local Elective Officials pursuant to COA Resolution No. 2011-014 dated December 13, 2011 and COA Memorandum No. 2012-008.
